methods.description: Process for selecting angiosperm leaf specimens from the latest Albian in the "La Dehesa" locality near Estercuel (Spain) based on the presence of evident plant-insect interactions, and their subsequent categorization and storage.

steps.description: For the present work, 142 angiosperm leaves from the latest Albian in the "La Dehesa" locality near Estercuel (Spain) were examined.
steps.searchable_summary: Examine a collection of fossil angiosperm leaves from a specific location and time period.
inputs: Collection of 142 angiosperm leaves (latest Albian, La Dehesa, Spain)

steps.description: Only specimens with evident plant-insect interactions-27 specimens-were selected for the study.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specimens exhibiting evident plant-insect interactions from the examined collection.

steps.description: 13 of which corresponded to leaves of genus Klitzschophyllites (specimens MAP-8346 to MAP-8358) 13 specimens were Angiosperm Type 1 (specimens MAP-8359 to MAP-8371), and one specimen was identified as Angiosperm Type 2 (specimen MAP-8372).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and categorize selected specimens by genus or type, assigning specimen numbers.
outputs: Categorized and numbered specimens (Klitzschophyllites, Angiosperm Type 1, Angiosperm Type 2)

steps.description: All selected samples were stored at the Museo Aragonés de Paleontología (MAP) at Fundación Conjunto Paleontológico de Teruel-Dinópolis (Teruel City, Spain).
steps.searchable_summary: Store selected and categorized specimens in a designated repository.

methods.description: Procedures for physically preparing fossil leaves to remove matrix material and then photographing them for documentation and analysis.

steps.description: The fossil leaves were prepared using a micro-pneumatic hammer and sharp needles under a stereomicroscope to remove the matrix material covering the fossils.
steps.searchable_summary: Physically prepare fossil leaves by removing matrix material using specific tools under a microscope.

steps.description: Photographs were taken using a Nikon D-90 camera with an AF-S Micro Nikkor 60 mm macro lens.
steps.searchable_summary: Capture high-resolution photographs of the prepared fossil leaves using specified camera equipment.

methods.description: Method for identifying and classifying observed damages on fossil leaves caused by arthropods using the standardized classification system proposed by [32]. This involves assigning damage types (DTs), functional feeding groups (FFGs), and assessing specialization.

steps.description: We used the classification system proposed by [32] to identify plant-arthropod interactions.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply a specific, referenced classification system to identify plant-arthropod interactions on fossil leaves.
inputs: Observed damages on fossil leaves (from prepared/imaged specimens)

steps.description: in which the damages observed in the leaves are classified into different morphotypes, termed damage types (DTs), and given an identifying number
steps.searchable_summary: Classify identified damages into distinct morphotypes (Damage Types, DTs) and assign a unique identifying number to each DT.
outputs: Classified damages with assigned DTs and numbers

steps.description: where the DTs belong to a particular functional feeding group (FFG).
steps.searchable_summary: Assign each identified Damage Type (DT) to a specific Functional Feeding Group (FFG).
inputs: FFG definitions from the classification system
outputs: Classified damages with assigned DTs and FFGs

steps.description: Additionally, each type of DT was identified with a certain degree of specialization based on its distribution on different taxa of host plants.
steps.searchable_summary: Assess the degree of specialization for each Damage Type (DT) based on its distribution across different host plant taxa.
inputs: Classified damages with DTs and FFGs
Data on host plant taxa for each observed DT
outputs: Assessed specialization degree for each DT
